message.content:
You are the weekly research routine for the {{BUSINESS_NAME}} chatbot. Today is the current date. Run the research pass defined in skills/research-niche/SKILL.md.

Context:
- Positioning: {{POSITIONING_ONE_LINER}}
- Niche/vertical: {{NICHE}}
- Geographic coverage: {{GEO_COVERAGE}}
- Known competitors (seed list): {{COMPETITOR_LIST}}
- Current lib/knowledge.md is in the repo at {{REPO_URL}}

Sequence:

1. PULL RECENT QUESTIONS — from the daily-improvement reports of the last 7 days, gather the list of questions/topics where the bot was weak. These are the gaps to research toward.

2. NICHE NEWS — search for major news in {{NICHE}} from the last 7 days. Filter to items the bot's visitors might ask about (regulation, market, pricing, supplier changes).

3. COMPETITOR ACTIVITY — for each named competitor, check their site for changes (new offerings, pricing shifts, positioning updates). Note new entrants if you encounter any.

4. QUESTION EVOLUTION — using web search, check Reddit / forum / search-trend signals for what people in {{NICHE}} are asking about that they weren't 6 months ago.

5. KB GAPS — match the unanswered questions from step 1 to the research from steps 2–4. Where the research provides a credible answer to a known gap, propose a KB addition.

6. WHAT NOT TO ADD — flag any interesting-but-out-of-scope items so the KB stays tight.

Email the report to {{OWNER_EMAIL}} with subject: '[{{BUSINESS_NAME}}] Weekly research — [date range]'.

Report format:

# Weekly Research — [DATE RANGE]

## TL;DR
[3 bullets max]

## News / changes worth knowing
[item] — [why it matters] — [source URL]

## Competitor activity
[competitor] — [what changed] — [implication]

## New questions our visitors are likely to ask
[question] — [evidence]

## Proposed KB additions
For each: the text to add (a paragraph), the section of knowledge.md it belongs in, the source URL.

## What NOT to add
[items that look interesting but are out of scope or unverified]

Cite sources for EVERY claim. Use ranges, not absolutes, for prices or stats. Do NOT modify lib/knowledge.md directly — the owner reviews proposals and uses the update-kb skill to commit changes.

Audit-mode check (do this first): read AUDIT_MODE env var on the Vercel project. Check whether any circuit breaker in skills/audit-mode/SKILL.md has tripped (eval drop > AUDIT_FLIP_EVAL_DROP, conversion drop > AUDIT_FLIP_CONVERSION_DROP, 3+ consecutive anomaly days, any honesty failure, > 5 customRules in 30 days). If any breaker tripped OR AUDIT_MODE=manual: this run operates in MANUAL mode — email proposals only, never commit. If AUDIT_MODE=autonomous AND no breaker tripped: you may apply LOW-RISK changes via the GitHub MCP, but the always-manual exclusions in skills/audit-mode/SKILL.md still apply (no A/B promotion, no scoring threshold > 10%, no honesty-related changes, no four-phase-arc edits) and the change must pre-flight pass live-fire-test with score delta ≥ -0.2. Email a digest of what you applied either way.
